old_claim.note: Merchant Arbitration Boards provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: The muda relied on maneuverable armed galleys, extending a long-established advanced galley-building lineage into state-organized commerce.

source_shape.source_locator: Galley-trade section: describes maneuverable armed galleys traveling in convoy and the state system that set destinations and sailing dates, made permanent for the muda in 1329.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Venice made its state-scheduled system of armed merchant-galley convoys a permanent institution in 1329.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The replacement follows the mechanism documented for the scoped milestone. The source directly describes armed maneuverable galleys as the vehicles of the convoy; arbitration boards do not organize vessel movement.

ontology_before: merchant_convoy_systems depended on the broad or mismatched prerequisite merchant_arbitration_boards.
ontology_after: merchant_convoy_systems now depends on advanced_shipbuilding_bireme_trireme, which matches the directly documented mechanism.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-12-structural-accuracy-batch: merchant_convoy_systems depends on advanced_shipbuilding_bireme_trireme and no longer on merchant_arbitration_boards.
would_reject_if: A reliable source shows merchant_arbitration_boards is indispensable while advanced_shipbuilding_bireme_trireme is absent from the current merchant_convoy_systems scope.
short_reason: The source directly describes armed maneuverable galleys as the vehicles of the convoy; arbitration boards do not organize vessel movement.
